info.title: Vatel AI REST API
info.description: Programmatic access for agents, telephony, and session tokens. Send `Authorization: Bearer <organization API key>` on all requests unless noted. Most error responses return JSON with an `error` string message.

tags.name: organization
tags.description: Organization tied to your API key

tags.name: session
tags.description: Short-lived session tokens and the API voice WebSocket (`GET /v1/connection`) opened with those credentials.

tags.name: calls
tags.description: Call history and recordings for your organization

get.description: Resolve id and name for the organization that owns the API key.
Returns sorted identifiers you may use for an agent's primary and fallback models. Values are `provider/model` strings.
Combined catalog of voices you can reference in agent `voice_settings`. The list may vary by environment and provider availability.
All agents owned by the organization for the current API key.

post.description: Creates a new agent; optional fields override server defaults.

404.description: Agent or agent version not found

post.description: Sets published_at on the version, sets this version as the agent's active version, and returns the updated version. Returns 409 if the version was already published.

404.description: Agent or agent version not found

post.description: Places an outbound call. Without `sipTrunkId`, uses the agent's assigned Twilio number and requires the agent to be active with a phone number. With `sipTrunkId`, dials via the assigned SIP outbound trunk (async); `number` or `destination` is then required. `number` and `destination` are aliases for the callee; send one or both with the same value.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Callee to dial (alias of `destination`). Required for a successful dial unless you rely solely on `destination`. Must match `destination` if both are sent.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Callee to dial (alias of `number`). Required for a successful dial unless you rely solely on `number`. Must match `number` if both are sent.

parameters.name: sipTrunkId
parameters.description: UUID of a SIP trunk assigned to this agent. When set, outbound uses the SIP trunk path instead of Twilio.

parameters.name: callerId
parameters.description: When using `sipTrunkId`, optional SIP From user override; defaults to the agent id.

parameters.name: firstMessage
parameters.description: Override the agent's first message for this call.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Override the agent's system prompt for this call.

200.description: Call request accepted (Twilio) or SIP dial accepted asynchronously (trunk path).

400.description: Missing or conflicting callee (`number`/`destination`), invalid UUIDs, invalid number length, or agent has no phone number on the Twilio path.

401.description: Missing Bearer token or invalid organization API key for this agent

403.description: SIP trunk not assigned to the agent, trunk organization mismatch, or related trunk access denial.

404.description: Agent, inactive agent, phone number record, or SIP trunk not found

503.description: Media or SIP credentials required for the trunk dial path are not configured on the server.

get.description: Paginated calls for the organization tied to your API key. Filter with optional query parameters. When using the internal server API key (`Authorization: Bearer` or `API-KEY` matching server config), you must send `organization_id` as a query parameter (UUID).

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization UUID. Required when the request is authenticated with the internal server API key; ignored for organization API keys.

parameters.name: agent_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated agent UUIDs to restrict results.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filter calls with `created_at` on or after this instant (RFC3339 or ISO8601 string accepted by the backend).

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filter calls with `created_at` on or before this instant.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Substring match on party number (max 256 characters).

parameters.name: outcome
parameters.description: Filter by termination outcome / reason bucket.

400.description: Invalid query parameters (e.g. invalid UUIDs or filter enums)

get.description: Returns one call by id if it belongs to an organization you can access.

404.description: Call not found or not accessible

get.description: Streams the WAV recording for the call (`{id}.wav` in storage). Response body is raw audio with `Content-Disposition: attachment`. Errors are JSON.

404.description: Call not found, not accessible, or recording object missing

400.description: Validation or Twilio business rule error

502.description: Bad gateway when the telephony provider returns an error

409.description: Inbound SIP username already in use

503.description: SIP connectivity is not available in this environment

get.summary: List agent assignments on SIP trunk

200.description: Assignments: `number` for digest-auth trunks, `alternate_number` for ACL trunks (globally unique).

post.summary: Create agent assignment on SIP trunk
post.description: Maps an org agent to this trunk. Use `number` when inbound auth is digest (unique on this trunk). Use `alternate_number` when inbound auth is ACL (globally unique alternate dial target). Same rules as the dashboard integrations UI.

400.description: Validation error or agent not in organization

404.description: SIP trunk or agent not found

409.description: Number or alternate number already in use

patch.description: Patch `number` and/or `alternate_number`. Send `null` to clear a field. Omitted keys are left unchanged. Assignment id is globally unique.

409.description: Number or alternate number already in use

400.description: Validation error or no fields to update

409.description: Inbound SIP username already in use

post.description: Short-lived session JWT in `token` to connect to an agent. For programmatic voice over WebSocket, open `GET /v1/connection` with query `token`, `agentId`, and optional `versionId` after receiving `token` here. Send `agent_id` and optional `transport`: `websocket` (default) or `webrtc` (adds `webrtc_token`, `room`, `identity`, `url`). Optional: `version_id`, `prompt`, `first_message`, and `chat` to include the `chat` scope alongside `voice` in the JWT.

400.description: Invalid JSON, disallowed extra properties, missing or invalid `agent_id`, invalid `version_id`, or invalid `transport`.

503.description: `webrtc` was requested but WebRTC media credentials or endpoint are not configured on the server.

get.description:
HTTP GET upgrades to a WebSocket (RFC 6455). Use the `token` from `POST /v1/session-token` and the same agent id.

**Query:** `token` (required), `agentId` (required, UUID), `versionId` (optional, UUID).

**Wire format:** UTF-8 JSON objects with `type` and `data`.

**Server → client** message types are documented under `VoiceSessionServerMessage` and its `oneOf` branches in components.

**Client → server:** `input_audio` with base64 PCM16 audio chunks (`VoiceSessionClientInputAudioMessage`), or `tool_call_output` after a server `tool_call` (`VoiceSessionClientToolCallOutputMessage`). Sample rate matches the session (24 kHz for the API transport).

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Agent UUID; must match the agent encoded in the session token.

101.description: Switching Protocols — connection becomes a WebSocket; subsequent messages are JSON as documented in `VoiceSessionServerMessage` and client message schemas.

400.description: Missing or invalid query parameters, or token validation failed.

403.description: Agent not allowed for this organization.

bearerAuth.description: Send your organization API key as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

LLMModel.description: Language model id in `provider/model` form. Use `GET /v1/llms` for values accepted on create and update.

TTSStrategy.description: Text-to-speech vendor backing the agent voice.

TimeoutAction.description: What happens when a timeout condition fires.

VoiceSettings.description: Voice and TTS provider for the agent. On create or update, include `id` and `provider`; optional tuning fields (when sent and non-zero) must be between 0 and 2.

id.description: Voice id from the vendor catalog (`GET /v1/voices`).

speed.description: Speech rate tuning when supported by the provider.

VadSettings.description: Voice-activity detection timing for turn-taking. On PATCH, send only the fields to change; omitted properties keep their stored values.

start_secs.description: Seconds of speech before a turn is considered started.

silence_timeout_secs.description: Silence duration that triggers end-of-turn or timeout behavior.

level.description: Noise suppression strength from 0 to 100 (percent), matching Krisp and the product UI.

TimeoutSettings.description: Limits and escalation rules for user silence and overall call duration.

default_timeout.description: Overall inactivity or session timeout (seconds), upper bound enforced server-side.

transfer_number.description: E.164 or carrier-accepted number when action is transfer.

transfer_message.description: Spoken or played message before transfer, when applicable.

silence_counter.description: Repeated silence checks or strikes before silence timeout.

silence_transfer_number.description: Transfer target when silence action is transfer.

Agent.description: Voice agent configuration for your organization. `llm` is the primary model; `fallback_llm` is used when the primary cannot serve.

phone_number_id.description: Assigned Twilio number id for this agent, if any. Empty when unassigned.

prompt.description: System or base instructions for the model.

first_message.description: Opening line the agent speaks when a call starts.

first_message_interruption_time.description: Window (seconds) where the first message can be interrupted.

default_language.description: Primary speech language: `multi`, `en`, `es`, `fr`, `de`, `it`, `pt`, `ru`, `zh`, `ja`, `ko`, `ar`, or `hi` (same set as the builder UI). `multi` is only appropriate for multilingual STT (e.g. Deepgram Nova).

summarize_calls.description: Whether to generate summaries after calls.

enable_first_message_outbound.description: Play `first_message` on outbound calls when true.

keyterms.description: Hints for transcription or keyword boosting, up to server limits.

AgentCreateInput.description: Fields accepted when creating an agent; server defaults apply to omitted values.

phone_number_id.description: Twilio number id belonging to this org, or null to leave unassigned.

default_language.description: One of: multi, en, es, fr, de, it, pt, ru, zh, ja, ko, ar, hi (see Agent.default_language).

keyterms.description: Optional list; empty omitted uses server default.

AgentUpdateInput.description: Partial update: only send fields to change. `keyterms`, when present, replaces the entire list.

phone_number_id.description: New assigned number id, or null to clear assignment.

default_language.description: One of: multi, en, es, fr, de, it, pt, ru, zh, ja, ko, ar, hi (see Agent.default_language).

Organization.description: The organization resolved from your API key.

LLMStringsResponse.description: Allowlisted model ids for agent LLM fields.

VoiceCatalogEntry.description: A single TTS voice offered by a provider.

id.description: Use this value in agent `voice_settings.id` with matching `provider`.

languages.description: Language codes supported by this voice.

GraphVersion.description: An agent version (tagged snapshot of the conversation flow).

tag.description: Optional label (e.g. draft or release name).

GraphNode.description: One node in the agent version (type-specific payload, edges, and metadata).

GraphVersionDetail.description: An agent version plus all of its nodes.

TwilioPhoneNumber.description: A Twilio number linked to your organization for voice agents.

id.description: App record id for this import.

account_sid.description: Twilio account this number belongs to.

TwilioPhoneNumberImportInput.description: Credentials to register a number with the platform; store secrets securely on the client until sent.

label.description: Optional; defaults to a generic label when empty.

auth_token.description: Twilio auth token for the account (used once at import).

CallerTransformType.description: How to adjust caller id before routing.

SIPTrunkAuthType.description: How inbound SIP is authenticated. `acl`: carrier traffic must match the ACL; `inbound_host` is the ACL-validated host. `digest`: SIP digest authentication. `none`: no inbound auth configured.

RegistrationStatus.description: Last known SIP registration outcome for the trunk leg.

SipTrunkCallerIDTransform.description: Rule applied to caller id on this trunk.

value2.description: Secondary operand when the transform requires it.

number.description: Numeric parameter (e.g. digit count) when applicable.

SipTrunk.description: SIP connectivity profile for a supported PBX integration.

pbx.description: PBX vendor preset (`3cx`, `yeastar`, `webex`) applying default trunk behavior, or `generic` for other / custom PBX setups where you supply hosts and credentials explicitly.

caller_id_transforms.description: Ordered transforms applied to caller id.

inbound_host.description: Inbound SIP host or domain. When `inbound_auth_type` is `acl`, this is the host validated against the ACL (allowed source for inbound calls).

inbound_sip_username.description: SIP user for inbound registration if used.

outbound_host.description: SIP host used for outbound legs: platform-initiated calls, on-call flows, and transfers dial out via this host (your carrier or SBC).

outbound_sip_username.description: Credentials username for outbound, if any.

register.description: Whether the platform registers this trunk with the carrier.

remain_in_dialog.description: Keeping SIP dialog routing behavior when true.

SipTrunkCreateInput.description: Create a SIP trunk; passwords are write-only on the wire.

pbx.description: PBX vendor preset or `generic` for other/custom PBX configurations.

inbound_host.description: Inbound SIP host or domain; when inbound auth is `acl`, this host is ACL-validated for inbound traffic.

outbound_host.description: Host to dial for outbound calls, on-call, and transfers.

SipTrunkUpdateInput.description: Patch only fields you need; empty string clears optional text fields per server rules.

pbx.description: Vendor preset or `generic` for other/custom PBX.

inbound_host.description: Inbound host; with `acl` auth, must match the ACL-validated host.

outbound_host.description: Outbound dial host for calls, on-call, and transfers.

SipTrunkAgentAssignment.description: Binds an org agent to a SIP trunk. With digest inbound auth, routing uses `number`. With ACL inbound auth, routing uses `alternate_number` (globally unique).

number.description: Number to dial for this agent when the trunk uses digest inbound auth; must be unique among assignments on this trunk.

alternate_number.description: Globally unique alternate dial string when the trunk uses ACL inbound auth; used instead of per-trunk `number` routing in that mode.

SipTrunkAgentAssignmentCreateInput.description: Create assignment. Use `number` for digest-auth trunks (unique on this trunk). Use `alternate_number` for ACL trunks (globally unique). Both may be set if you use both routing styles.

number.description: Target number to dial for digest inbound auth; unique among assignments on this SIP trunk.

alternate_number.description: Globally unique alternate number to dial when the trunk uses ACL inbound auth.

SipTrunkAgentAssignmentPatchInput.description: Patch only fields present in the JSON. Use null to clear `number` or `alternate_number`.

number.description: Digest-auth routing number (unique per trunk); null clears.

alternate_number.description: ACL routing alternate dial string (globally unique); null clears.

agent_id.description: UUID of the agent that will run this voice session.

version_id.description: Optional published version UUID. When omitted, the server uses the agent's resolved active version.

chat_id.description: Optional chat UUID to embed into the session token claims (`parameters.chat_id`) for downstream chat continuity.

transport.description: `websocket`: platform WebSocket session (default when omitted). `webrtc`: WebRTC media room; response adds `room`, `identity`, and `url`.

first_message.description: Optional opening line override for this session (WebSocket session token and WebRTC JWT participant attributes when applicable).

prompt.description: Optional system prompt override for this session (WebSocket session parameters and WebRTC JWT participant attributes when applicable).

chat.description: When true, the `token` includes the `chat` scope in addition to `voice`, authorizing the chat API.

SessionTokenResponse.description: Session JWT in `token`; for `webrtc`, also `webrtc_token`, `room`, `identity`, and `url`.

token.description: Platform session JWT. `scopes` claim includes `voice`, and `chat` when chat was requested. For API voice WebSocket, pass as query `token` on `GET /v1/connection` with `agentId`. Also use as `Authorization: Bearer` for the chat endpoint where applicable.

room.description: Media room name assigned by the server. Present only when `transport` was `webrtc`.

identity.description: Participant identity assigned by the server for the WebRTC session. Present only when `transport` was `webrtc`.

url.description: WebSocket URL of the media server. Present only when `transport` was `webrtc`.

webrtc_token.description: LiveKit media-room access JWT (microphone subscribe and publish; participant attributes include `agent_id` and, when provided, `version_id`, `prompt`, and `first_message`). Present only when `transport` was `webrtc`.

DialAgentResponse.description: Acknowledgement that an outbound call was requested (Twilio) or that a SIP trunk dial was accepted to run asynchronously.

success.description: True when the provider accepted the call request.

CallSource.description: How the call entered the platform.

ContextVariable.description: Extracted or session variable attached to a call.

value.description: JSON value; shape depends on dataType.

TranscriptToolCall.description: Tool invocation metadata on a transcript row.

TranscriptEntry.description: One line in the call transcript (message, tool call, tool output, or interruption).

role.description: For message rows: user, assistant, or system. May be empty for tool or interruption rows.

CallFeedback.description: Optional post-call feedback stored as JSON.

Call.description: Public call record. `cost` is the customer-facing (marked-up) total; raw provider cost is not exposed.

transcript.description: Conversation transcript. Omitted when not stored for the call.

cost.description: Marked-up total cost for the call.

duration_seconds.description: Approximate duration from started_at to ended_at when both are set.

feedback.description: Caller feedback; omitted when not set.

id.description: Call / session id for this connection.

VoiceSessionResponseAudioData.description: `data` when `type` is `response_audio` (agent TTS chunk, base64 PCM).

turn_id.description: Turn or playback context id for this audio stream.

is_final.description: True when this is the last chunk for this utterance or segment.

turn_id.description: Turn id associated with this assistant message.

text.description: Assistant text (may repeat or refine while `is_final` is false).

is_final.description: True when this text is committed for the turn.

VoiceSessionInputAudioTranscriptData.description: `data` when `type` is `input_audio_transcript` (user speech transcript from STT).

is_final.description: False for interim hypotheses; true for final transcript.

emulated.description: True when the start was synthesized for pipeline coordination rather than raw VAD.

VoiceSessionEmptyData.description: Empty object for signaling-only server message types.

VoiceSessionInputVariable.description: Argument binding on a client tool call.

dataType.description: Value shape hint (string, number, boolean, etc.).

VoiceSessionClientToolCallData.description: `data` when `type` is `tool_call` (server requests client-side tool execution).

VoiceSessionServerMessage.description: Single JSON WebSocket message from server to client on `GET /v1/connection`. Shape is always `{ "type": string, "data": object }`.

VoiceSessionClientInputAudioMessage.description: Client → server: streamed user microphone audio.

VoiceSessionClientToolCallOutputMessage.description: Client → server: result for a prior `tool_call`.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error body for most non-2xx JSON responses on this API.

error.description: Human-readable message; may include validation detail.
